prompt: Add full-text search using Elasticsearch for product listings

turns.content:
<task>
Add full-text search for product listings using Elasticsearch.
</task>

src/
  models/
    product.py
  services/
    product_service.py
  api/
    products.py
  config.py

1	 from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Float, Text
2	 from database import Base
3	 
4	 class Product(Base):
5	     __tablename__ = 'products'
6	     id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
7	     name = Column(String(200))
8	     description = Column(Text)
9	     category = Column(String(100))
10	     price = Column(Float)

arguments.query: elasticsearch-py Python client search index document example

turns.content:
1. elasticsearch-py docs - https://elasticsearch-py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
   Official Python client for Elasticsearch.
2. Elasticsearch Python Quickstart - https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/client/python-api/current/getting-started-python.html
   pip install elasticsearch. Connect, index, search examples.

Getting started with Elasticsearch Python client

pip install elasticsearch

from elasticsearch import Elasticsearch
es = Elasticsearch('http://localhost:9200')

# Index a document
es.index(index='products', id=1, document={'name': 'Widget', 'description': '...'})

# Search
result = es.search(index='products', query={
    'multi_match': {
        'query': 'search term',
        'fields': ['name^2', 'description']  # name boosted 2x
    }
})
hits = result['hits']['hits']  # [{_id, _source, _score}, ...]

# Bulk index
from elasticsearch.helpers import bulk
actions = [{'_index': 'products', '_id': p.id, '_source': {...}} for p in products]
bulk(es, actions)

arguments.title: Elasticsearch Python client usage
arguments.content:
pip install elasticsearch
es = Elasticsearch('http://localhost:9200')
Index: es.index(index='name', id=id, document={...})
Search: es.search(index='name', query={'multi_match': {'query': q, 'fields': ['name^2', 'description']}})
Bulk: from elasticsearch.helpers import bulk; bulk(es, actions)
Hits: result['hits']['hits'] → [{_id, _source, _score}]
Field boosting: 'name^2' means name matches worth 2x

turns.content: Finding recorded: Elasticsearch Python client usage

arguments.summary: Researched Elasticsearch Python client. Will create search service with indexing and multi_match search, boosting product name over description.

turns.content: Added content after line 2 in src/config.py

arguments.message: Search service is ready. I've set up field boosting: name is 3x, category is 2x, description is 1x. Fuzzy matching is enabled for typo tolerance. There's also a bulk_index method for initial data loading. Do you want me to add the search API endpoint too?

arguments.summary: Created SearchService with Elasticsearch integration. Supports single and bulk indexing, multi_match search with field boosting (name 3x, category 2x) and fuzzy matching.
arguments.final_answer: Elasticsearch search service created with index_product, search, and bulk_index methods. Search uses multi_match with field boosting (name 3x, category 2x) and fuzzy matching for typo tolerance. Set ELASTICSEARCH_URL env var.
